Program_Description: WIC is a health and nutrition program for babies and children under 5, pregnant women, and new mothers.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children under 5
Eligibility_Requirements: Have low to medium income; and/or receive Medi-Cal, TANF, or CalFresh benefits.
Application_Process: Express interest using the online form or call for an appointment.
Required_Documentation: Proof of California residency is required.

Program_Description: The MCAH program empowers and educates parents and families each step of the way to improve the well-being of the kids in our community.
Target_Population: Pregnant parents and families with young kids
Application_Process: Call or email to sign up

Program_Description: A free, in-home cognitive behavioral therapy program for Nevada County parents who are experiencing depression during or after pregnancy.
Target_Population: Parents experiencing postpartum depression or anxiety
Eligibility_Requirements: Must be enrolled in one of the local home visiting programs
Application_Process: Talk to your home visitor or contact the MCAH program

Program_Description: A Home Visitor from Healthy Babies provides support and guidance to new and expecting parents.
Application_Process: Visit their website or fill out a contact form

Program_Description: A program that helps pregnant and parenting teens stay healthy and succeed in school.
Eligibility_Requirements: Must be 19 years old or younger
